chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/2)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/2)

parts.text: يَحْيَى بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي الْبِلَادِ

text: الصَّبْرُ وَ الرِّضَا عَنِ اللَّهِ رَأْسُ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ وَ مَنْ صَبَرَ وَ رَضِيَ عَنِ اللَّهِ فِيمَا قَضَى عَلَيْهِ فِيمَا أَحَبَّ أَوْ كَرِهَ لَمْ يَقْضِ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ فِيمَا أَحَبَّ أَوْ كَرِهَ إِلَّا مَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Yahya Bin Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Balaad, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly,

(It has been narrated) from Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The patience and the satisfaction from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is the chief of the obedience to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and the one who is patient and satisfied from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> regarding whatever has been Judged upon him, with regards to whatever he loves or abhors, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would not Judge for him regarding whatever he loves or abhors, except what is better for him’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is narrated from him from Yahya ibn Ibrahim ibn abu al-Balad from ‘Asim ibn Humayd from abu Hamza al-Thumali from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “Patience and agreeing with the decisions of Allah is the head of obedience to Allah. If one exercises patience and agrees with the decisions of Allah in all the matters which he loves or dislikes, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will not make any decision in any of his affairs which he loves or dislikes but that the decisions are in his best interest.”
